message: Hi B1! I'm looking to sell at a fair price. My costs are quite high, so I'll need a strong offer. I'm thinking somewhere around 95 would work for me. What range are you considering?
I'm willing to pay up to 96, but 95 is too high for me. Let's find a lower price that works for both of us.
I paid 93 last round. My max is 96, but I need a better deal. Can you lower your ask? I'll bid higher if you ask much lower.
Sounds fair. I'll ask 87 if you bid 92+. That price works better for both of us than last round.
Last round's 90 price was good. I can bid 91 if you ask 86. That gives us both profit. Let's keep trading.
Ask 88 for me if you can bid 91. Fair split between us. Let's keep this going!
90 has been a fair price for both of us. Let's stick with it - I'll ask 90, you bid 91. We both profit every round. Consistency is key over 17 more rounds!
90 works but 91 bid leaves me little profit. I'll bid 90 if you ask 86. That gives us both more profit per trade over many rounds. Let's agree 90/86.
90 has worked well for 3 rounds - we both profit consistently. Let's keep it simple: I'll ask 88, you bid 92, midpoint stays at 90. Reliable profits for both of us over 16 more rounds.
90 price is fine but 92 bid leaves me only 4 profit. I'll bid 91 if you ask 87, midpoint 89 gives us both more profit per trade over many rounds. Let's agree 91/87.
89 price works well. I'll bid 90 if you ask 88. That gives us both steady profit for many rounds. Let's lock this in.
Perfect! I'll ask 89 if you bid 91—midpoint 90. That's our best mutual profit rate like round 1. Let's lock this deal for all remaining rounds!
